title: Advanced topics
text: section reproduces complete pipeline Getting Started vignette single self-contained block, demonstrating every best practice: leakage-free context construction, targets defined context filtering, competing-risk fine-tune two outcomes.
code: library(RSurvivEHR)  # ── 1. Data (same population as the Getting Started vignette) ──────────────── events_pop <- data.frame(   patient_id = c(rep(1,4), rep(2,6), rep(3,6), rep(4,4), rep(5,6),                  rep(6,4), rep(7,4), rep(8,4), rep(9,6), rep(10,4)),   event = c(     "HYPERTENSION","STATIN","BP_CHECK","CVD",     "HYPERTENSION","BP_CHECK","T2D","METFORMIN","HYPERTENSION","CVD",     "HYPERTENSION","BP_CHECK","STATIN","T2D","BP_CHECK","CVD",     "HYPERTENSION","STATIN","T2D","METFORMIN",     "HYPERTENSION","BP_CHECK","T2D","HBA1C","METFORMIN","STATIN",     "HYPERTENSION","AMLODIPINE","BP_CHECK","STATIN",     "STATIN","T2D","HBA1C","METFORMIN",     "HYPERTENSION","BP_CHECK","STATIN","T2D",     "HYPERTENSION","BP_CHECK","T2D","METFORMIN","HBA1C","STATIN",     "STATIN","BP_CHECK","HYPERTENSION","T2D"   ),   age = c(     55.0, 55.5, 56.2, 58.0,     44.0, 45.5, 48.0, 48.3, 50.5, 52.0,     58.0, 59.5, 62.0, 63.5, 64.0, 65.5,     45.0, 45.5, 47.0, 48.3,     48.0, 49.0, 51.0, 52.0, 53.0, 54.5,     60.0, 61.0, 62.3, 63.0,     40.0, 42.0, 43.5, 44.8,     58.0, 59.2, 60.0, 62.0,     46.0, 47.5, 50.0, 52.0, 52.5, 54.0,     44.0, 46.5, 47.0, 48.5   ),   value = c(     NA,  NA,  148, NA,     NA,  145, NA,  NA,  NA,  NA,     NA,  158, NA,  NA,  162, NA,     NA,  NA,  NA,  NA,     NA,  152, NA,  68,  NA,  NA,     NA,  NA,  155, NA,     NA,  NA,  74,  NA,     NA,  145, NA,  NA,     NA,  138, NA,  NA,  71,  NA,     NA,  138, NA,  NA   ) )  static_pop <- data.frame(   patient_id    = 1:10,   SEX           = c("M","F","M","F","M","F","M","F","M","F"),   ETHNICITY     = c("White","Asian","White","Black","White",                     "Asian","White","White","Black","White"),   IMD           = c(3L, 1L, 5L, 2L, 4L, 3L, 1L, 5L, 2L, 4L),   YEAR_OF_BIRTH = c(1960L,1970L,1952L,1975L,1963L,1958L,1978L,1960L,1968L,1975L) )  # ── 2. Config ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # time_scale = 5.0 → 5-year prediction window; stored in pretrained bundle # and inherited automatically at fine-tune and prediction time. cfg <- survivehr_config(   block_size = 64, n_layer = 2, n_head = 2, n_embd = 64,   epochs = 10, batch_size = 4,   surv_layer = "competing-risk",   time_scale = 5.0 )  # ── 3. Pre-train on all 10 patients ─────────────────────────────────────────── pt <- survivehr_pretrain(events_pop, static_pop, cfg) # Vocabulary ordered by frequency (most common first): # <PAD>=0, <UNK>=1, HYPERTENSION=2, BP_CHECK=3, T2D=4, STATIN=5, ... pt$event_vocab  survivehr_save_model(pt, "pretrain_backbone.pt")  # ── 4. Fine-tune: competing-risk CVD vs T2D (patients 1–6) ─────────────────── ft_static <- static_pop[static_pop$patient_id %in% 1:6, ]  # Define targets FIRST — the outcome age sets the context cut-off targets <- data.frame(   patient_id   = c(1L,    2L,    3L,    4L,    5L,    6L),   target_event = c("CVD", "T2D", "T2D", "T2D", "T2D", "STATIN"),   target_age   = c(58.0,  48.0,  63.5,  47.0,  51.0,  63.0) ) # p1: CVD case.  p2–p5: T2D occurred first (CVD is the competing event for p2/p3). # p6: censored — neither CVD nor T2D observed.  # Build context: remove both outcome codes AND events after the target age ft_events <- events_pop[events_pop$patient_id %in% 1:6 &                            !events_pop$event %in% c("CVD", "T2D"), ] ft_events <- merge(ft_events, targets[, c("patient_id", "target_age")], by = "patient_id") ft_events <- ft_events[ft_events$age < ft_events$target_age,                         c("patient_id", "event", "age", "value")]  ft <- survivehr_finetune(   events            = ft_events,   targets           = targets,   outcomes          = c("CVD", "T2D"),   # two competing outcomes   risk_model        = "competing-risk",   static_covariates = ft_static,   config            = cfg,   pretrained_model  = pt   # vocabulary, time_scale, and weights all inherited )  # ── 5. Predict on the full cohort (outcome codes removed from context) ──────── pred_events <- events_pop[!events_pop$event %in% c("CVD", "T2D"), ] preds <- survivehr_predict(ft, pred_events, static_pop) # Columns: patient_id, CVD_cdf_last, CVD_auc, T2D_cdf_last, T2D_auc preds  # ── 6. Save / load round-trip ───────────────────────────────────────────────── tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".pt") survivehr_save_model(ft, tmp) ft2 <- survivehr_load_model(tmp) identical(ft2$event_vocab, ft$event_vocab)  # TRUE unlink(tmp)

code: # 10-patient pre-training cohort. # Patients 1–3 have CVD events so the vocabulary includes CVD — # a prerequisite for CVD fine-tuning in Section 5. events_pop <- data.frame(   patient_id = c(rep(1,4), rep(2,6), rep(3,6), rep(4,4), rep(5,6),                  rep(6,4), rep(7,4), rep(8,4), rep(9,6), rep(10,4)),   event = c(     "HYPERTENSION","STATIN","BP_CHECK","CVD",                          # p1 — CVD at 58     "HYPERTENSION","BP_CHECK","T2D","METFORMIN","HYPERTENSION","CVD",  # p2 — T2D at 48, CVD at 52     "HYPERTENSION","BP_CHECK","STATIN","T2D","BP_CHECK","CVD",         # p3 — T2D at 63.5, CVD at 65.5     "HYPERTENSION","STATIN","T2D","METFORMIN",                         # p4 — T2D at 47     "HYPERTENSION","BP_CHECK","T2D","HBA1C","METFORMIN","STATIN",      # p5 — T2D at 51     "HYPERTENSION","AMLODIPINE","BP_CHECK","STATIN",                   # p6 — censored     "STATIN","T2D","HBA1C","METFORMIN",                                # p7 — T2D at 42     "HYPERTENSION","BP_CHECK","STATIN","T2D",                          # p8 — T2D at 62     "HYPERTENSION","BP_CHECK","T2D","METFORMIN","HBA1C","STATIN",      # p9 — T2D at 50     "STATIN","BP_CHECK","HYPERTENSION","T2D"                           # p10 — T2D at 49   ),   age = c(     55.0, 55.5, 56.2, 58.0,     44.0, 45.5, 48.0, 48.3, 50.5, 52.0,     58.0, 59.5, 62.0, 63.5, 64.0, 65.5,     45.0, 45.5, 47.0, 48.3,     48.0, 49.0, 51.0, 52.0, 53.0, 54.5,     60.0, 61.0, 62.3, 63.0,     40.0, 42.0, 43.5, 44.8,     58.0, 59.2, 60.0, 62.0,     46.0, 47.5, 50.0, 52.0, 52.5, 54.0,     44.0, 46.5, 47.0, 48.5   ),   value = c(     NA,  NA,  148, NA,          # p1 : BP_CHECK = 148 mmHg     NA,  145, NA,  NA,  NA, NA, # p2 : BP_CHECK = 145 mmHg     NA,  158, NA,  NA,  162,NA, # p3 : BP_CHECK = 158 mmHg, 162 mmHg     NA,  NA,  NA,  NA,          # p4     NA,  152, NA,  68,  NA, NA, # p5 : BP_CHECK = 152 mmHg, HBA1C = 68 mmol/mol     NA,  NA,  155, NA,          # p6 : BP_CHECK = 155 mmHg     NA,  NA,  74,  NA,          # p7 : HBA1C = 74 mmol/mol     NA,  145, NA,  NA,          # p8 : BP_CHECK = 145 mmHg     NA,  138, NA,  NA,  71, NA, # p9 : BP_CHECK = 138 mmHg, HBA1C = 71 mmol/mol     NA,  138, NA,  NA           # p10: BP_CHECK = 138 mmHg   ) )

title: Getting started with RSurvivEHR
text: One row per patient. Categorical columns (< 80 % numeric) one-hot encoded automatically; numeric columns pass unchanged. Column naming: patient_id reserved name. Every column can named freely — backend encodes whatever finds. contrast, events targets tables require fixed column names (patient_id, event, age, value; patient_id, target_event, target_age, target_value respectively). Important: always pass columns order across pretrain, fine-tune, prediction. model records exact encoded column list training time raises clear error prediction data match.
code: static_pop <- data.frame(   patient_id    = 1:10,   SEX           = c("M","F","M","F","M","F","M","F","M","F"),   ETHNICITY     = c("White","Asian","White","Black","White",                     "Asian","White","White","Black","White"),   IMD           = c(3L, 1L, 5L, 2L, 4L, 3L, 1L, 5L, 2L, 4L),   YEAR_OF_BIRTH = c(1960L,1970L,1952L,1975L,1963L,1958L,1978L,1960L,1968L,1975L) )

title: Getting started with RSurvivEHR
text: Run validators training call catch schema problems early — wrong column names, unsorted ages, non-numeric values — clear R error rather cryptic Python traceback.
code: survivehr_validate_events(events_pop) survivehr_validate_static(static_pop) # [OK] Events: 48 rows, 10 patients. Columns present, ages numeric and time-ordered. # [OK] Static covariates: 10 patients, 4 covariate column(s): SEX, ETHNICITY, IMD, YEAR_OF_BIRTH.

title: Getting started with RSurvivEHR
text: survivehr_config() returns named list consumed training functions. Key parameters:
code: cfg <- survivehr_config(   block_size    = 64,   # context window — sequences are padded/truncated to this   n_layer       = 2,    # transformer blocks   n_head        = 2,    # attention heads (n_embd must be divisible by n_head)   n_embd        = 64,   # embedding / hidden dimension   dropout       = 0,    # dropout rate (increase for regularisation)   learning_rate = 3e-4,   epochs        = 10,   batch_size    = 4,   surv_layer    = "competing-risk",  # or "single-risk" — see Section 5   time_scale    = 5.0   # prediction window = 5 years (ages are in years) )

title: Getting started with RSurvivEHR
text: Pre-training builds vocabulary trains backbone transformer full event history.
code: pt_model <- survivehr_pretrain(   events            = events_pop,   static_covariates = static_pop,   config            = cfg )  # Vocabulary ordered by frequency — most-common events get the smallest token IDs. # With this dataset: HYPERTENSION (10), BP_CHECK (9), STATIN (9), T2D (8), # METFORMIN (5), CVD (3), HBA1C (3), AMLODIPINE (1). pt_model$event_vocab

title: Getting started with RSurvivEHR
text: Save backbone now can load later fine-tune different outcome definitions without re-training scratch.
code: survivehr_save_model(pt_model, "pretrain_backbone.pt")  # Reload at any time — vocabulary, time_scale, and token policy are all preserved pt_model <- survivehr_load_model("pretrain_backbone.pt")

title: Getting started with RSurvivEHR
text: Fine-tuning requires targets data frame labelling, patient, outcome observed (last observed non-outcome event censored). Two leakage conditions must satisfied building context: Remove outcome event codes context — outcome supplied targets. Remove events outcome age — events occurred labelled outcome available prediction time. condition 2 requires knowing patient’s target_age, define targets filtering context. use patients 1–6 fine-tuning cohort.
code: ft_static <- static_pop[static_pop$patient_id %in% 1:6, ] survivehr_validate_static(ft_static) # [OK] Static covariates: 6 patients, 4 covariate column(s): SEX, ETHNICITY, IMD, YEAR_OF_BIRTH.

title: Getting started with RSurvivEHR
text: Use risk_model = "competing-risk" two outcomes compete — .e. occurrence one prevents observed. model CVD vs T2D: patients 1–6 experience exactly one two outcomes first, censored neither occurred. outcome codes removed context neither leaks input features.
code: # 1. Define targets first — the outcome age is needed to cut off the context targets_cr <- data.frame(   patient_id   = c(1L,    2L,    3L,    4L,    5L,    6L),   target_event = c("CVD", "T2D", "T2D", "T2D", "T2D", "STATIN"),   target_age   = c(58.0,  48.0,  63.5,  47.0,  51.0,  63.0) ) survivehr_validate_targets(targets_cr)  # 2. Build context: patients 1–6, outcome codes removed, events after outcome age excluded ft_events_cr <- events_pop[events_pop$patient_id %in% 1:6 &                               !events_pop$event %in% c("CVD", "T2D"), ] ft_events_cr <- merge(ft_events_cr, targets_cr[, c("patient_id", "target_age")],                       by = "patient_id") ft_events_cr <- ft_events_cr[ft_events_cr$age < ft_events_cr$target_age,                               c("patient_id", "event", "age", "value")] survivehr_validate_events(ft_events_cr) # [OK] Events: 15 rows, 6 patients. ...  # time_scale is not set here — it is inherited automatically from the pretrained bundle cfg_cr <- survivehr_config(   block_size = 64, n_layer = 2, n_head = 2, n_embd = 64,   dropout = 0, learning_rate = 3e-4, epochs = 10, batch_size = 4,   surv_layer = "competing-risk" )  ft_cr <- survivehr_finetune(   events            = ft_events_cr,   targets           = targets_cr,   outcomes          = c("CVD", "T2D"),   # two competing outcomes   risk_model        = "competing-risk",   static_covariates = ft_static,   config            = cfg_cr,   pretrained_model  = pt_model           # vocabulary + weights inherited )  cat("Fine-tune (CR) loss history:", unlist(ft_cr$history), "\n")

title: Getting started with RSurvivEHR
text: Use risk_model = "single-risk" single endpoint interest competing events need modelled explicitly. model CVD : patients 1–3 CVD cases; patients 4–6 right-censored controls.
code: # 1. Define targets first targets_sr <- data.frame(   patient_id   = c(1L,    2L,    3L,     4L,          5L,       6L),   target_event = c("CVD", "CVD", "CVD",  "METFORMIN", "STATIN", "STATIN"),   target_age   = c(58.0,  52.0,  65.5,   48.3,         54.5,     63.0) ) survivehr_validate_targets(targets_sr)  # 2. Build context: CVD removed, events after outcome age excluded # (T2D remains in context because it is not an outcome in this single-risk model) ft_events_sr <- events_pop[events_pop$patient_id %in% 1:6 &                               events_pop$event != "CVD", ] ft_events_sr <- merge(ft_events_sr, targets_sr[, c("patient_id", "target_age")],                       by = "patient_id") ft_events_sr <- ft_events_sr[ft_events_sr$age < ft_events_sr$target_age,                               c("patient_id", "event", "age", "value")] survivehr_validate_events(ft_events_sr) # [OK] Events: 24 rows, 6 patients. ...  # time_scale is not set here — it is inherited automatically from the pretrained bundle cfg_sr <- survivehr_config(   block_size = 64, n_layer = 2, n_head = 2, n_embd = 64,   dropout = 0, learning_rate = 3e-4, epochs = 10, batch_size = 4,   surv_layer = "competing-risk" )  ft_sr <- survivehr_finetune(   events            = ft_events_sr,   targets           = targets_sr,   outcomes          = "CVD",             # single outcome   risk_model        = "single-risk",   static_covariates = ft_static,   config            = cfg_sr,   pretrained_model  = pt_model )  cat("Fine-tune (SR) loss history:", unlist(ft_sr$history), "\n")

title: Getting started with RSurvivEHR
code: survivehr_save_model(ft_cr, "model_cr.pt") survivehr_save_model(ft_sr, "model_sr.pt")  # Reload — weights, vocabulary, static column schema, time_scale and device are preserved ft_cr2 <- survivehr_load_model("model_cr.pt") ft_sr2 <- survivehr_load_model("model_sr.pt")

title: Getting started with RSurvivEHR
text: Prediction uses full 10-patient population (including patients 7–10 held fine-tuning) demonstrate population-level risk scoring. Two preparation steps required — logic fine-tuning: Remove outcome event codes context. Remove events patient’s prediction age — patients known outcome (p1–p6) target_age; held-patients (p7–p10) last recorded event age.
code: # Prediction ages: target_age for p1-6 (from targets_cr / targets_sr), # last recorded event age for p7-10. pred_ages_cr <- data.frame(   patient_id = c(1L,   2L,   3L,    4L,   5L,   6L,   7L,   8L,   9L,  10L),   pred_age   = c(58.0, 48.0, 63.5, 47.0, 51.0, 63.0, 44.8, 62.0, 54.0, 48.5) ) pred_ages_sr <- data.frame(   patient_id = c(1L,   2L,   3L,    4L,   5L,   6L,   7L,   8L,   9L,  10L),   pred_age   = c(58.0, 52.0, 65.5, 48.3, 54.5, 63.0, 44.8, 62.0, 54.0, 48.5) )  # Competing-risk context: remove CVD + T2D, keep events before pred_age pred_events_cr <- events_pop[!events_pop$event %in% c("CVD", "T2D"), ] pred_events_cr <- merge(pred_events_cr, pred_ages_cr, by = "patient_id") pred_events_cr <- pred_events_cr[pred_events_cr$age < pred_events_cr$pred_age,                                   c("patient_id", "event", "age", "value")] survivehr_validate_events(pred_events_cr) # [OK] Events: 27 rows, 10 patients. ...  # Single-risk context: remove CVD only, keep events before pred_age pred_events_sr <- events_pop[events_pop$event != "CVD", ] pred_events_sr <- merge(pred_events_sr, pred_ages_sr, by = "patient_id") pred_events_sr <- pred_events_sr[pred_events_sr$age < pred_events_sr$pred_age,                                   c("patient_id", "event", "age", "value")] survivehr_validate_events(pred_events_sr) # [OK] Events: 38 rows, 10 patients. ...  # Competing-risk predictions — CVD and T2D risk head preds_cr <- survivehr_predict(ft_cr2, pred_events_cr, static_pop) head(preds_cr) # Columns: patient_id, CVD_cdf_last, CVD_auc, T2D_cdf_last, T2D_auc  # Single-risk predictions — CVD risk head preds_sr <- survivehr_predict(ft_sr2, pred_events_sr, static_pop) head(preds_sr) # Columns: patient_id, CVD_cdf_last, CVD_auc

title: Getting started with RSurvivEHR
text: Pass new patients exactly static columns used training time. model stores exact encoded column list (including one-hot expanded categories) raises clear error rather silently producing wrong predictions mismatch. See Model architecture article full configuration reference official Gadd et al. 2025 hyperparameter table.
code: new_events <- data.frame(   patient_id = c(99, 99, 99, 99),   event      = c("HYPERTENSION", "BP_CHECK", "METFORMIN", "ASPIRIN"),  # ASPIRIN → <UNK>   age        = c(55.0,  55.3,       55.4,         56.1),   value      = c(NA,    158,        NA,           NA) )  # Must include the same columns as static_pop: SEX, ETHNICITY, IMD, YEAR_OF_BIRTH new_static <- data.frame(   patient_id    = 99,   SEX           = "M",   ETHNICITY     = "White",   IMD           = 2L,   YEAR_OF_BIRTH = 1963L )  pred_new <- survivehr_predict(ft_cr2, new_events, new_static) print(pred_new)

text: Gadd, C. et al. (2025). SurvivEHR: Transformer-based survival analysis electronic health records. medRxiv. doi:10.1101/2025.08.04.25332916

title: Model architecture and parameter reference
text: SurvivEHR GPT-style transformer pre-trained sequences clinical events (diagnoses, prescriptions, measurements, …) via causal language modelling. pre-training model “understands” temporal structure medical histories. neural-ODE survival head fine-tuned labelled outcome data (target event age event) produce calibrated time--event curves.
code: EHR sequences --> Tokeniser --> Token + positional embedding                                         |                             n_layer x NeoGPT block  (MHA + MLP + LayerNorm)                                         |                             Last attended hidden state                                         |                 +-----------------------+----------------------+                 |  Competing-risk ODE head (one ODE per event)|                 |  or                                         |                 |  Single-risk ODE head (one ODE)             |                 +---------------------------------------------+                                         |                             CDF curves per time horizon

title: Model architecture and parameter reference
text: following table reproduces hyperparameters used original CPRD study. values target large dataset GPU resources. Note context window (fine-tuning): fine-tuning block size extended 512 patient’s context formed last unique occurrence event rather allowing repeated measurements. Global diagnoses (conditions recorded context window) appended context rather dropped.

title: Model architecture and parameter reference
code: library(RSurvivEHR)  cfg <- survivehr_config(   # -- Transformer architecture -------------------------------------------   block_size    = 128,       # context window (tokens); upstream pre-train: 256, fine-tune: 512   n_layer       = 4,         # transformer blocks;      upstream: 6   n_head        = 4,         # attention heads;         upstream: 6   n_embd        = 256,       # embedding dimension;     upstream: 384    # -- Regularisation ------------------------------------------------------   dropout       = 0.0,       # applied before attention and after MLP    # -- Optimisation --------------------------------------------------------   learning_rate = 3e-4,      # AdamW learning rate; upstream pre-train backbone: 3e-4   epochs        = 1,         # upstream pre-train: 10;  fine-tune: 20   batch_size    = 16,        # upstream pre-train: 64;  fine-tune: 512    # -- Survival head -------------------------------------------------------   surv_layer    = "competing-risk",  # or "single-risk"   surv_weight   = 1.0,   value_weight  = 0.0,    # -- Tokenisation --------------------------------------------------------   include_unk     = TRUE,   include_cls_sep = FALSE,    # -- Age / time handling -------------------------------------------------   time_scale    = 1.0,       # divide all raw ages by this number    # -- Hardware ------------------------------------------------------------   device        = "auto" )

title: Model architecture and parameter reference
text: context window maximum number tokens (clinical events) seen model . Sequences longer block_size right-truncated – recent events retained. Sequences shorter block_size left-padded <PAD> tokens (index 0). Upstream pre-training used block_size = 256 repeated measurements (e.g. two BMI records retained). Fine-tuning extended 512 switched last-unique context (keeping recent record event type) plus global diagnoses appended start. Tip: set block_size 95th percentile sequence lengths dataset.

title: Model architecture and parameter reference
text: block consists multi-head self-attention (MHA), two-layer MLP, LayerNorm (Pre-LN), residual connections. layers gives greater representational capacity requires data compute.

title: Model architecture and parameter reference
text: SurvivEHR pre-training goes beyond standard causal language modelling (predicting event comes next). backbone also trains survival head predict next event occurs, using age sequence position time target. surv_layer controls timing problem framed: Used pre-training fine-tuning — backbone constructor reads cfg.head.SurvLayer stages, architecture must remain consistent. outcome-level head produces patient predictions controlled separately risk_model argument survivehr_finetune(). entirely separate nn.Module objects, surv_layer risk_model can differ — e.g. pretrain surv_layer = "competing-risk" fine-tune risk_model = "single-risk" conflict. upstream config uses abbreviated codes "cr" "sr" – also accepted RSurvivEHR.

title: Model architecture and parameter reference
text: Upstream used surv_weight = 1, value_weight = 0.1 models also learned measurement magnitudes (e.g. BMI, blood pressure).

title: Model architecture and parameter reference
text: Pre-training . setting fixed vocabulary built survivehr_pretrain() stored bundle. value fine-tune config ignored. Reserves <UNK> (index 1). unseen event code fine-tune / prediction time silently mapped <UNK>.

title: Model architecture and parameter reference
text: Pre-training . Fixed vocabulary build time stored bundle. value fine-tune config ignored. Set TRUE BERT / BEHRT-style sequences. used upstream GPT-style pre-training.

title: Model architecture and parameter reference
text: raw ages divided time_scale entering model. Must consistent across pre-training, fine-tuning, prediction. Pre-training: used age normalisation — prediction window stage. Fine-tuning: also defines prediction window. ODE integrates [0,1][0,1] corresponds [0,𝚝𝚒𝚖𝚎_𝚜𝚌𝚊𝚕𝚎][0, \texttt{time\_scale}] raw time units, time_scale = 5.0 means survival curves cover 5-year window. value stored pretrained bundle reused automatically — need repeat fine-tune config.

title: Model architecture and parameter reference
text: RSurvivEHR always uses NeoGPT block (block_type = "Neo"): Rotary Position Embeddings (RoPE) inside attention. SwiGLU activation MLP. Pre-LN (LayerNorm sub-layer).

title: Model architecture and parameter reference
text: competing-risk head, one ODE instantiated per outcome event, encoder MLP, lightweight ODE right-hand-side network, sigmoid CIF output. single-risk head, single ODE produces one survival curve.

title: Model architecture and parameter reference
text: Built automatically survivehr_pretrain(): Unique event codes collected events data frame. <PAD> -> index 0 (always; padding_idx = 0) <UNK> -> index 1 (include_unk = TRUE) <CLS> -> index 2 (include_cls_sep = TRUE) <SEP> -> index 3 (include_cls_sep = TRUE) Clinical tokens assigned indices >= 2 (>= 4 CLS/SEP), ordered frequency — common event first (lowest index). fine-tuning, outcome codes absent vocab automatically appended embedding layer extended using weight-safe copy pre-trained backbone.

title: RSurvivEHR — Transformer Survival Models for EHR Data

Categorical columns one-hot encoded automatically; numeric columns pass unchanged. Continuous readings (blood pressure, HbA1c, BMI, etc.) go value column alongside event recorded . Rows without reading NA.

code: survivehr_config(   block_size = 128,   n_layer = 4,   n_head = 4,   n_embd = 256,   dropout = 0,   learning_rate = 3e-04,   epochs = 1,   batch_size = 16,   surv_layer = "competing-risk",   surv_weight = 1,   value_weight = 0,   device = "auto",   include_unk = TRUE,   include_cls_sep = FALSE,   time_scale = 1 )

title: Build an RSurvivEHR configuration list — survivehr_config
text: block_size Sequence length padding/truncation. n_layer Number transformer blocks. n_head Number attention heads. n_embd Hidden embedding size. dropout Dropout probability. learning_rate Learning rate. epochs Number epochs. batch_size Batch size. surv_layer Pretrain survival head: "competing-risk" "single-risk". surv_weight Survival loss weight. value_weight Value regression loss weight. device "auto", "cpu", "cuda". include_unk Whether reserve use <UNK> unseen events. include_cls_sep Whether add <CLS> <SEP> around sequence. time_scale Controls age normalisation length prediction window.  Every raw age divided value entering model; survival ODE evaluates normalised [0, 1] grid maps back [0, time_scale] original age units. Use 5.0 5-year prediction window ages years (recommended). Use 1.0 1-year window ages years. Use 365.25 1-year window ages days. Use 1826.25 5-year window ages days. Stored automatically every model bundle; need supply prediction time. Must across pretrain, fine-tune, prediction.

what: Fine-tune the RSurvivEHR backbone on labelled outcomes — survivehr_finetune
title: Fine-tune the RSurvivEHR backbone on labelled outcomes — survivehr_finetune
text: Attaches fresh outcome-level survival head top pre-trained backbone fine-tunes combined model labelled cohort.  Two head types supported: "competing-risk" — models two outcomes compete (first occur prevents others observed).  Supply outcome codes outcomes. "single-risk" — models single endpoint; patients without outcome treated right-censored.

title: Fine-tune the RSurvivEHR backbone on labelled outcomes — survivehr_finetune
code: survivehr_finetune(   events,   targets,   outcomes,   risk_model = c("competing-risk", "single-risk"),   static_covariates = NULL,   config = survivehr_config(),   pretrained_model = NULL,   event_vocab = NULL )

title: Fine-tune the RSurvivEHR backbone on labelled outcomes — survivehr_finetune
text: events data.frame context events (outcome codes post-outcome rows removed) columns patient_id, event, age, optionally value. targets data.frame columns patient_id, target_event, target_age labelling observed outcome (cases) last non-outcome event (censored patients).  Build survivehr_validate_targets(). outcomes Character vector outcome event codes fine-tuned head predicts.  Must match codes used targets. risk_model "competing-risk" (default) "single-risk". Controls architecture outcome-level head — independent surv_layer used pre-training. static_covariates optional data.frame columns used pre-training time.  Pass NULL omit. config named list survivehr_config().  time_scale inherited pre-trained bundle pretrained_model supplied — need set . pretrained_model Model bundle returned survivehr_pretrain() survivehr_load_model().  supplied, vocabulary, weights, time_scale inherited bundle. event_vocab optional named integer vector overriding vocabulary.  Rarely needed; prefer supplying pretrained_model.

title: Fine-tune the RSurvivEHR backbone on labelled outcomes — survivehr_finetune
text: avoid data leakage, events frame passed must: outcome event codes removed (supplied via targets). Contain events occurred patient's target_age (events outcome available prediction time).

title: Fine-tune the RSurvivEHR backbone on labelled outcomes — survivehr_finetune
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Uses events_pop / static_pop / cfg / pt from survivehr_pretrain() example. ft_static <- static_pop[static_pop$patient_id %in% 1:6, ]  # ---- Competing-risk: CVD vs T2D (patients 1-6) --------------------------- targets_cr <- data.frame(   patient_id   = c(1L,    2L,    3L,    4L,    5L,    6L),   target_event = c("CVD", "T2D", "T2D", "T2D", "T2D", "STATIN"),   target_age   = c(58.0,  48.0,  63.5,  47.0,  51.0,  63.0) ) survivehr_validate_targets(targets_cr)  # Remove both outcomes; keep only events before target_age ft_events_cr <- events_pop[events_pop$patient_id %in% 1:6 &                               !events_pop$event %in% c("CVD","T2D"), ] ft_events_cr <- merge(ft_events_cr,                       targets_cr[, c("patient_id","target_age")],                       by = "patient_id") ft_events_cr <- ft_events_cr[ft_events_cr$age < ft_events_cr$target_age,                               c("patient_id","event","age","value")]  cfg_cr <- survivehr_config(   block_size = 64, n_layer = 2, n_head = 2, n_embd = 64,   epochs = 10, batch_size = 4, surv_layer = "competing-risk"   # time_scale inherited automatically from the pretrained bundle ) ft_cr <- survivehr_finetune(   events = ft_events_cr, targets = targets_cr,   outcomes = c("CVD","T2D"), risk_model = "competing-risk",   static_covariates = ft_static, config = cfg_cr, pretrained_model = pt ) cat("CR loss:", unlist(ft_cr$history), "\n")  # ---- Single-risk: CVD only (patients 1-6) -------------------------------- targets_sr <- data.frame(   patient_id   = c(1L,    2L,    3L,    4L,          5L,       6L),   target_event = c("CVD", "CVD", "CVD", "METFORMIN", "STATIN", "STATIN"),   target_age   = c(58.0,  52.0,  65.5,  48.3,         54.5,     63.0) ) survivehr_validate_targets(targets_sr)  # Remove CVD only; keep only events before target_age ft_events_sr <- events_pop[events_pop$patient_id %in% 1:6 &                               events_pop$event != "CVD", ] ft_events_sr <- merge(ft_events_sr,                       targets_sr[, c("patient_id","target_age")],                       by = "patient_id") ft_events_sr <- ft_events_sr[ft_events_sr$age < ft_events_sr$target_age,                               c("patient_id","event","age","value")]  cfg_sr <- survivehr_config(   block_size = 64, n_layer = 2, n_head = 2, n_embd = 64,   epochs = 10, batch_size = 4, surv_layer = "competing-risk" ) ft_sr <- survivehr_finetune(   events = ft_events_sr, targets = targets_sr,   outcomes = "CVD", risk_model = "single-risk",   static_covariates = ft_static, config = cfg_sr, pretrained_model = pt ) cat("SR loss:", unlist(ft_sr$history), "\n") } # }

title: Pre-train the RSurvivEHR backbone transformer — survivehr_pretrain
text: Builds event vocabulary supplied event history, pre-trains transformer backbone competing-risk single-risk survival head predicts next clinical event occur (vocabulary tokens).  resulting model bundle can passed directly survivehr_finetune() saved survivehr_save_model() later reuse.

title: Pre-train the RSurvivEHR backbone transformer — survivehr_pretrain
text: events data.frame columns patient_id, event, age (optionally value).  Validated survivehr_validate_events() passed Python. static_covariates optional data.frame patient_id covariate columns.  Categorical columns one-hot encoded automatically; numeric columns pass unchanged.  Pass NULL (default) train without static features. config named list survivehr_config() specifying architecture training hyperparameters. event_vocab optional named integer vector fixing token mapping.  Useful pre-training multiple models must share vocabulary.  NULL (default) builds vocabulary supplied events, ordered descending frequency.

title: Pre-train the RSurvivEHR backbone transformer — survivehr_pretrain
text: named list (model bundle) elements: model trained PyTorch model object. event_vocab Named integer vector mapping event codes token IDs (frequency-descending order, common = smallest ID). inv_vocab Reverse mapping token IDs event codes. config configuration used training. time_scale prediction window / age normalisation divisor stored bundle. token_policy Token policy flags (include_unk, include_cls_sep). history List per-epoch training losses. device String identifying compute device used (e.g. "cpu" "cuda:0").

title: Pre-train the RSurvivEHR backbone transformer — survivehr_pretrain
text: Pre-training require targets frame — next-event age patient's raw sequence serves supervision signal.

title: Pre-train the RSurvivEHR backbone transformer — survivehr_pretrain
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # ---- Pre-training on the 10-patient population from the Getting Started #      vignette.  Patients 1-3 have CVD in their history so the vocabulary #      includes CVD — required for CVD fine-tuning later. events_pop <- data.frame(   patient_id = c(rep(1,4), rep(2,6), rep(3,6), rep(4,4), rep(5,6),                  rep(6,4), rep(7,4), rep(8,4), rep(9,6), rep(10,4)),   event = c(     "HYPERTENSION","STATIN","BP_CHECK","CVD",     "HYPERTENSION","BP_CHECK","T2D","METFORMIN","HYPERTENSION","CVD",     "HYPERTENSION","BP_CHECK","STATIN","T2D","BP_CHECK","CVD",     "HYPERTENSION","STATIN","T2D","METFORMIN",     "HYPERTENSION","BP_CHECK","T2D","HBA1C","METFORMIN","STATIN",     "HYPERTENSION","AMLODIPINE","BP_CHECK","STATIN",     "STATIN","T2D","HBA1C","METFORMIN",     "HYPERTENSION","BP_CHECK","STATIN","T2D",     "HYPERTENSION","BP_CHECK","T2D","METFORMIN","HBA1C","STATIN",     "STATIN","BP_CHECK","HYPERTENSION","T2D"   ),   age = c(     55.0, 55.5, 56.2, 58.0,     44.0, 45.5, 48.0, 48.3, 50.5, 52.0,     58.0, 59.5, 62.0, 63.5, 64.0, 65.5,     45.0, 45.5, 47.0, 48.3,     48.0, 49.0, 51.0, 52.0, 53.0, 54.5,     60.0, 61.0, 62.3, 63.0,     40.0, 42.0, 43.5, 44.8,     58.0, 59.2, 60.0, 62.0,     46.0, 47.5, 50.0, 52.0, 52.5, 54.0,     44.0, 46.5, 47.0, 48.5   ),   value = c(     NA,  NA,  148, NA,     NA,  145, NA,  NA,  NA,  NA,     NA,  158, NA,  NA,  162, NA,     NA,  NA,  NA,  NA,     NA,  152, NA,  68,  NA,  NA,     NA,  NA,  155, NA,     NA,  NA,  74,  NA,     NA,  145, NA,  NA,     NA,  138, NA,  NA,  71,  NA,     NA,  138, NA,  NA   ) ) static_pop <- data.frame(   patient_id    = 1:10,   SEX           = c("M","F","M","F","M","F","M","F","M","F"),   ETHNICITY     = c("White","Asian","White","Black","White",                     "Asian","White","White","Black","White"),   IMD           = c(3L, 1L, 5L, 2L, 4L, 3L, 1L, 5L, 2L, 4L),   YEAR_OF_BIRTH = c(1960L,1970L,1952L,1975L,1963L,1958L,1978L,1960L,1968L,1975L) ) # 5-year prediction window; ages are in years cfg <- survivehr_config(   block_size = 64, n_layer = 2, n_head = 2, n_embd = 64,   epochs = 10, batch_size = 4, time_scale = 5.0 ) pt <- survivehr_pretrain(events_pop, static_pop, cfg) # Vocabulary is frequency-ordered: most-common events get the smallest IDs # e.g. <PAD>=0, <UNK>=1, HYPERTENSION=2, BP_CHECK=3, STATIN=4, T2D=5, ... pt$event_vocab } # }

title: RSurvivEHR 0.7.8
text: Consistent 10-patient example dataset throughout: R function @examples R/train.R now use 10-patient population (events_pop / static_pop) Getting Started vignette. patient realistic prior-history events (e.g. HYPERTENSION, BP_CHECK) outcome event, matching structure real EHR data. Competing-risk example now genuinely uses two outcomes: survivehr_finetune() example Getting Started vignette Section 5a now model CVD vs T2D (outcomes = c("CVD", "T2D"), risk_model = "competing-risk"). previous example used single outcome, incorrect competing-risk models. Leakage-free cohort construction examples: targets now defined filtering context events. merge--filter pattern (age < target_age) used consistently across vignette function examples, ensuring post-outcome events appear input. time_scale removed fine-tune configs examples: cfg_cr cfg_sr longer set time_scale (inherited automatically pretrained bundle). comment explains inheritance. survivehr_pretrain(), survivehr_finetune(), survivehr_predict(), survivehr_save_model(), survivehr_load_model(): full @description, @param, @return sections replacing one-liner stubs. survivehr_validate_events(), survivehr_validate_static(), survivehr_validate_targets(): detailed @param \describe{} item lists explaining column accepted aliases. survivehr_config(): description updated reference Model architecture vignette Gadd et al. (2025) hyperparameters. -memory bundle (ft_sr): survivehr_predict() iterated string character--character, labelling CDF column "C_cdf_last" instead "CVD_cdf_last". survivehr_save_model() / survivehr_load_model(): string persisted, load_model_bundle iterated "C", "V", "D" outcome names — none present vocabulary — outcome_tokens = [] reloaded head 0 risks, returning data frame patient_id. Fixed normalising outcomes proper list four sites survivehr_backend.py: train_finetune_model return, predict_next_events, save_model_bundle, load_model_bundle. Single-outcome single-risk models (common case) now correct paths. adjacent string literals without separating comma (valid R syntax). Replaced paste0() wrapping strings. DESCRIPTION description updated: removed outdated “FastEHR installation required” clause; added mentions biomarker value support, leakage-free helpers, frequency-ordered vocabularies. advanced-topics.Rmd full worked example aligned: standalone 6-patient dataset “Full worked example” section replaced 10-patient population used Getting Started, ensuring documentation consistent. README quick-start replaced prose: R code block README used different 5-patient dataset replaced prose “Getting started” section pointing readers three vignettes.

title: RSurvivEHR 0.7.7
text: 5-year prediction window: examples documentation now use time_scale = 5.0, giving 5-year prediction window ages years. survivehr_config() default remains 1.0 recommended value clinical applications 5.0. Clarified time_scale semantics: time_scale controls age normalisation divisor prediction window length. survival ODE evaluates normalised [0, 1] grid maps back [0, time_scale] raw age units. time_scale stored inside every model bundle; survivehr_predict() reads automatically — need supplied inference time. now documented survivehr_config() @param, survivehr_predict() @return, backend docstring, Getting Started vignette (Section 3 Section 7). Biomarker values examples: 10-patient population R/train.R examples vignettes/getting-started.Rmd now includes realistic value entries: BP_CHECK readings (138–162 mmHg) HBA1C readings (68–74 mmol/mol) aligned correct event rows. Removed FastEHR branding README.md: “FastEHR compatibility” row removed key-features table FastEHR alias description Input format section reworded plain English.

title: RSurvivEHR 0.7.6
text: Frequency-ordered vocabulary: event tokens now assigned IDs descending frequency order (common event = smallest ID reserved tokens <PAD>, <UNK>). Smaller IDs improve embedding compression common clinical codes. Existing saved models unaffected vocabulary stored inside every .pt bundle. Improved examples throughout: R function examples Getting Started vignette now use 10-patient pre-training cohort three CVD cases providing outcome token, 6-patient fine-tuning subset (3 CVD cases + 3 right-censored controls). Context events explicitly filtered remove outcome (leakage-free). Documented prediction outputs time window: survivehr_predict() @return now fully documents {outcome}_cdf_last (cumulative incidence end prediction window = time_scale time units) {outcome}_auc (area CDF = average risk window). vignette explains extend prediction window changing time_scale. Removed FastEHR branding: fastEHR / FastEHR mentions removed documentation, error messages, parameter descriptions. Uppercase column aliases (PATIENT_ID, EVENT, DAYS_SINCE_BIRTH) continue work described without FastEHR name.

text: Fixed static covariate validation new / small-batch patients: previous strict equality check one-hot encoded column names failed new patients pd.get_dummies single row produces dummy columns categories present row (e.g. SEX_M SEX_F). Validation now split two layers: – Raw column names (e.g. SEX, IMD, HEIGHT_CM) validated strictly — clear ValueError raised user passes wrong feature names. – Encoded (one-hot) columns aligned silently via reindex fill_value=0.0 — categories absent small prediction batch correctly filled zeros rather causing error. Terminology standardised across documentation: static covariate column names now consistently UPPER_CASE (SEX, ETHNICITY, SMOKING_STATUS, IMD, YEAR_OF_BIRTH) README, getting-started vignette, advanced-topics vignette, test.R. variable holding static data consistently named static_covariates (static). duplicate ## 2 section heading getting-started vignette corrected ## 3 Configuration. Columns present, ages numeric time-ordered. — users get positive confirmation rather silent invisible(TRUE). save_model_bundle / load_model_bundle now persist static_col_names .pt checkpoint column validation survives save/reload cycle.

title: RSurvivEHR 0.5.0
text: Updated vignette “Model architecture & parameter reference” official hyperparameter table Gadd et al. (2025). Now documents pre-training vs fine-tuning differences every parameter: block_size (256 vs 512), batch_size (64 vs 512), epochs (10 vs 20), backbone LR (3e-4 vs 5e-5), head LR (3e-4 vs 5e-4), scheduler (linear warmup + cosine annealing warm restarts vs ReduceLROnPlateau), early stopping (disabled vs enabled patience 30). Added context-window explanation: fine-tuning uses last-unique context global diagnoses appended. Added separate cfg_pretrain cfg_finetune recommended configs HPC / large-dataset users.

Updated Getting started vignette document fine-tuning modes separate sub-sections (§ 4a Competing-risk, § 4b Single-risk), comparison table updated prediction examples models.

title: RSurvivEHR 0.2.0
text: Added survivehr_setup() create populate RSurvivEHR Python virtual environment. Works macOS, Windows, Linux. Added NAMESPACE exports R/setup.R. Added transformers list required Python packages installed survivehr_setup() (needed src/models/TTE/base.py src/models/transformer/base.py use transformers.modeling_utils.ModuleUtilsMixin). title: RSurvivEHR 0.1.0
text: Full R interface SurvivEHR: survivehr_config(), survivehr_pretrain(), survivehr_finetune(), survivehr_predict(), survivehr_save_model(), survivehr_load_model(), survivehr_validate_events(), survivehr_validate_static(), survivehr_validate_targets(). Python backend vendored inside inst/python/ — FastEHR installation required. Explicit PAD / UNK / CLS / SEP token policy configurable via survivehr_config(). Global time_scale normalisation (default 1.0 ages years; use 1825.0 match FastEHR’s DAYS_SINCE_BIRTH convention). Automatic static covariate encoding: categorical columns → one-hot dummies; numeric columns → passed -. Competing-risk single-risk survival heads supported. Leakage-free fine-tuning: context sequence restricted events target event age. Accepts FastEHR column aliases (PATIENT_ID, EVENT, DAYS_SINCE_BIRTH).
